title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public
summary: A podcast discussion examining whether Google is falling behind OpenAI and Anthropic in AI capabilities, particularly around coding agents and super-apps. The episode explores how AI agents may reshape web browsing and app usage patterns, potentially disadvantaging Google's search and advertising model.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz and M.G. Siegler debate whether Google is ceding AI leadership to OpenAI and Anthropic, framing coding agents and super-apps as the emerging competitive advantages. The discussion covers how AI agents are reshaping web, email, and app usage, previews Apple's WWDC plans, and addresses Anthropic's IPO filing. Key threads: the case for and against Google falling behind, the strategic weight of coding agents, and what an Anthropic public listing signals.
